memoryArtifacts.headline: Brief Bio.
memoryArtifacts.description:
Harald IV Gille (c. 1103 – Dec. 14, 1136)  (Also known as Haraldr gilli or Haraldr gillikristr).“Gille” or “Gillikristr” may mean "servant of Christ".

Harald IV Gille was born ca. 1103 in, possibly, …

zh.Eidsberg, Østfold, Norway: 挪威奧斯特福爾德艾斯波格
zh.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille是Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt和Female Unknown的兒子，於1102年出生在愛爾蘭。他於大約1122年與Christine在瑞典結婚。他去世於1136年12月14日在挪威霍爾達藍德伯根享年34歲。

zh-hans.Eidsberg, Østfold, Norway: 挪威奧斯特福爾德艾斯波格
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille是Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt和Female Unknown的儿子，于1102年出生在爱尔兰。他于大约1122年与Christine在瑞典结婚。他去世于1136年12月14日在挪威霍爾達藍德伯根享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Magnusson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Niels, Anders, Erland, Gisli, Kerstin, Lars, Nils, Ottar, Thor.  German Hans, Kurt, Fritz.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name   Magnus  .
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, or North German   Magnussen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Magnus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Bernd, Egon, Erwin, Kurt, Otto, Reinhard, Volker, Wilhelm, Wolfgang.

German, Flemish, and Walloon: from the personal name Magnus, which is from the Latin adjective magnus ‘great’ or a Latinized form of a short form of ancient Germanic compound names based on the element magan ‘strength, might’. The name was borne by several early Christian saints, including St. Magnus (8th century), a monk in St. Gallen in Switzerland and missionary in Allgäu in southern Germany, whose real name was Magnoald. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). In Belgium, it may also be a Latinized (humanistic) form of Walloon Legrand .

English, Scottish, Swedish, Danish, Norwegian, and North German: from the Scandinavian personal name Magnus, borne by Magnus the Good (died 1047), king of Norway and Denmark, who was named for the Emperor Charlemagne, Latin Carolus Magnus ‘Charles the Great’, under the impression that Magnus was a personal name. The name spread from Norway to the eastern Scandinavian royal houses, and became popular all over Scandinavia and thence in the English Danelaw. It has also been adopted as a Jewish name.

variants.name: Magnuson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Nels, Helmer, Sven, Kerstin, Alf, Anders, Hilma, Knut, Monrad, Thor.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish   Magnusson  .
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, and North German   Magnussen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Manson
meanings: Scottish (mainly the Northern Isles): shortened form of   Magnusson  .

English: patronymic, probably sometimes from the Middle English personal name Man, Manne (Old English Mann(a) or Old Danish Manni) + -son; compare Mann . Occasionally perhaps from a Middle English personal name Mainstan (Old English Mægenstān, from mægen ‘might, power’ + stān ‘stone’).
Americanized form of Swedish Månsson (rarely Månson): patronymic from the personal name Måns, a derivative of Magnus . Compare Monson .

nameMeanings.name: Harald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Old Norse personal name Haraldr, meaning ‘army leader’, cognate with English Harold.

en.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille was born in 1102, in Ireland as the son of Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt and Female Unknown. He married Christine about 1122, in Sweden. He died on 14 December 1136, in Bergen, Hordaland, Norway, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille est né en 1102, à Irlande, fils de Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt et Female Unknown. Il a épousé Christine environ 1122, à Suède. Il est décédé le 14 décembre 1136, à Bergen, Hordaland, Norvège, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille wurde 1102 in Irland, als Sohn von Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt und Female Unknown geboren. Er hat um 1122 in Schweden Christine geheiratet. Er starb am 14. Dezember 1136 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Bergen, Hordaland, Norwegen.

it.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille è nato il nel 1102 nel Irlanda come figlio di Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt e Female Unknown. Lui sposato Christine circa 1122, nel Svezia. Morì il 14 dicembre 1136, nel Bergen, Hordaland, Norvegia, all'età di 34 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gilleは1102年にアイルランドでMagnus Olafsson III BerrføttとFemale Unknownの息子として生まれた。約1122年にスウェーデンでChristineと結婚した。彼は1136年12月14日にノルウェーホールダランドバージェンで34歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille(은)는 1102년 아일랜드에서 Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt(와)과 Female Unknown의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1122년 스웨덴에서 Christine(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1136년 12월 14일 노르웨이 호르다랜드 버겐에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille nasceu em 1102, em Irlanda filho de Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt e Female Unknown. Ele casou-se com Christine aproximadamente 1122, em Suécia. Ele faleceu em 14 de dezembro de 1136, em Bergen, Hordaland, Noruega, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille родился в 1102 г. в местности Ирландия. Его родителей звали Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt и Female Unknown. Он женился на женщине по имени Christine около 1122 г. в местности Швеция. Он умер 14 декабря 1136 г. в местности биржен, хаурдаланд, Норвегия в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Harald Magnusson IV Gille nació en 1102, en Irlanda como hijo de Magnus Olafsson III Berrføtt y Female Unknown. Se casó con Christine aproximadamente 1122, en Suecia. Murió el 14 de diciembre de 1136, en Bergen, Hordaland, Noruega, a la edad de 34 años.
